extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR won La Fleche Wallonne with a seated, late-accelerating attack on the steepest bend of Mur de Huy after UAE controlled all day.
tactical_patterns: UAE burned entire team controlling pace all day despite having single protected rider, risky all-in strategy paid off.
VERBEKKE Louis bridged breakaway solo then attacked again showing QuickStep willing to gamble pre-final climb despite slim odds.
BARDET Roman squeezed wide by WOODS on steepest ramp shows outside line disadvantage on narrow Mur finish.
POGACAR attacked seated on steepest bend, not before, indicating power advantage over explosive punch specialists on gradient.

rider_signals.SKELMOSA Matthias: positive: second place and closing margin on line suggests emerging Ardennes threat for Trek with multiple options
rider_signals.HEALY Ben: positive: positioned well deep into finale suggesting Israel developing genuine classics leader beyond Woods
rider_signals.VERBEKKE Louis: positive: double bridge effort from breakaway shows QuickStep has aggressive classics diesel beyond traditional Mur puncher profile
rider_signals.WOODS Mike: neutral: led steep ramp but squeezed Bardet wide suggesting over-commitment without finishing kick
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: fifth shows Trek diversified tactics beyond Skelmosa with two top-five finishers
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: neutral: burned doing leadout work for Ineos suggests miscast role rather than protected status
